response.justification: Fundamental mismatch with core requirements. The candidate has only ~6 months of professional C++ experience at National Instruments, falling drastically short of the mandatory 5–10 years. More critically, the entire career trajectory is web/fullstack development with zero exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial services—the domain is non-negotiable for a consulting engagement in this space. While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering practices and communication skills, the missing C++ depth, Windows platform expertise, and complete lack of financial domain knowledge make this unsuitable for a mid-senior capital markets role.
strengths: Demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals (TDD, CI/CD, code quality focus) across multiple roles
Excellent communication and teaching ability, evidenced by workshops, talks, and active open-source project maintenance
Proven capacity to learn diverse technical stacks and contribute across web, mobile, embedded, and DevOps domains
concerns: Only ~6 months of documented professional C++ experience (National Instruments) versus the mandatory 5–10 years requirement
Zero experience in capital markets, fixed income, derivatives, or financial services—no domain foundation for a banking/investment systems role
Career focused on web/fullstack development with Linux and NoSQL; lacks Windows platform expertise and SQL Server/Oracle database specialization
